perturbation.original_text: 1.39 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means with respect to [***].
perturbation.changed_text: 1.39 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means efforts comparable to those used for a similar product.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' is omitted and replaced with a much broader, less specific definition. This is contradictory because the term is referenced elsewhere in the contract, particularly in Section 3.4 and Section 4.2 regarding AbbVie's obligations to develop and commercialize the Licensed Product, but with the clause omitted, it introduces ambiguity and uncertainty about the actual required level of effort. This omission directly affects the enforceability of AbbVie's diligence obligations because the term 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' is used to define those obligations.

perturbation.changed_text: 1.108 "Net Sales" means gross sales less standard deductions.
perturbation.explanation: The original detailed definition of 'Net Sales' is removed, replaced with 'gross sales less standard deductions'. This creates a critical ambiguity because what constitutes 'standard deductions' is now undefined. This omission affects royalty payments, outlined in Section 6.5, creating uncertainty. The exact milestone payments and royalties that Harpoon is owed depends heavily on the definition of the 'Net Sales', with the new definition this term becomes less enforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 7.3.1 Enforcement of Harpoon Patents. Each Party shall promptly notify the other Party in writing of any alleged or threatened infringement of the Product-Specific Patents by a Third Party in the Territory of which such Party becomes aware based on the development, commercialization or Exploitation of, or an application to market, a Licensed Product or a product containing a Licensed Compound in the Territory (the "Product Infringement"). AbbVie shall have the sole right, but not the obligation, to prosecute any Product Infringement involving any claims of Product-Specific Patents at its sole expense and AbbVie shall retain control of the prosecution of such claim, suit or proceeding. Harpoon shall have the right to join as a party to such claim, suit, or proceeding in the Territory and participate with its own counsel at its own expense; provided that AbbVie shall retain control of the prosecution of such claim, suit, or proceeding. During any such claim, suit, or proceeding, AbbVie shall keep Harpoon reasonably informed of all material developments in connection with such claim, suit or proceeding. If AbbVie does not take commercially reasonable steps to prosecute (including settling) such a Product Infringement in a country or jurisdiction, then (a) Harpoon may, but is not obligated to, prosecute the Product Infringement at its own expense in such country or jurisdiction, and (b) if Harpoon prosecutes such Product Infringement and obtains an injunction that prevents the sale of a Biosimilar Product by such Third Party in such country or jurisdiction, AbbVie shall not be entitled to apply any royalty reductions pursuant to Section 6.5.3(a) that would otherwise apply as a result of the sale of such Biosimilar Product by such Third Party after the period of such injunction.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.3.1 Enforcement of Harpoon Patents. Each Party shall promptly notify the other Party in writing of any alleged or threatened infringement of the Product-Specific Patents by a Third Party in the Territory of which such Party becomes aware based on the development, commercialization or Exploitation of, or an application to market, a Licensed Product or a product containing a Licensed Compound in the Territory (the "Product Infringement"). AbbVie shall have the sole right, but not the obligation, to prosecute any Product Infringement involving any claims of Product-Specific Patents at its sole expense and AbbVie shall retain control of the prosecution of such claim, suit or proceeding. Harpoon shall have the right to join as a party to such claim, suit, or proceeding in the Territory and participate with its own counsel at its own expense; provided that AbbVie shall retain control of the prosecution of such claim, suit, or proceeding. During any such claim, suit, or proceeding, AbbVie shall keep Harpoon reasonably informed of all material developments in connection with such claim, suit or proceeding. If AbbVie does not take commercially reasonable steps to prosecute (including settling) such a Product Infringement in a country or jurisdiction, then Harpoon may, but is not obligated to, prosecute the Product Infringement at its own expense in such country or jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: I have omitted the section that states: (b) if Harpoon prosecutes such Product Infringement and obtains an injunction that prevents the sale of a Biosimilar Product by such Third Party in such country or jurisdiction, AbbVie shall not be entitled to apply any royalty reductions pursuant to Section 6.5.3(a) that would otherwise apply as a result of the sale of such Biosimilar Product by such Third Party after the period of such injunction. Due to this the AbbVie and Harpoon can be in disadvantage, because even if Harpoon is able to win the lawsuit, if there is a biosimilar, they may have royalty reductions, which is contradictory to when they are fighting for their IP.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.3.1 Enforcement of Harpoon Patents. Each Party shall promptly notify the other Party in writing of any alleged or threatened infringement of the Product-Specific Patents by a Third Party in the Territory of which such Party becomes aware based on the development, commercialization or Exploitation of, or an application to market, a Licensed Product or a product containing a Licensed Compound in the Territory (the "Product Infringement"). AbbVie shall have the sole right, but not the obligation, to prosecute any Product Infringement involving any claims of Product-Specific Patents at its sole expense and AbbVie shall retain control of the prosecution of such claim, suit or proceeding. Harpoon shall have the right to join as a party to such claim, suit, or proceeding in the Territory and participate with its own counsel at its own expense; provided that AbbVie shall retain control of the prosecution of such claim, suit, or proceeding. During any such claim, suit, or proceeding, AbbVie shall keep Harpoon reasonably informed of all material developments in connection with such claim, suit or proceeding. If AbbVie does not take commercially reasonable steps to prosecute (including settling) such a Product Infringement in a country or jurisdiction, then Harpoon may, but is not obligated to, prosecute the Product Infringement at its own expense in such country or jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that if Harpoon successfully prosecutes a Product Infringement and obtains an injunction against a biosimilar product, AbbVie will not be entitled to apply royalty reductions. The changed text removes this clause, creating a contradiction. Without this protection, even if Harpoon wins the lawsuit, AbbVie might still apply royalty reductions, undermining Harpoon's efforts and creating a disadvantage that directly conflicts with the intent of protecting their IP, as implicitly stated in the surrounding paragraphs. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, since it exists in the same section, it still indicates there is a contradiction, because if the original text was to still exist, it would be contradictory to the changed text.
